threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' and not 'the Overground' when you hop from Dalston Junction to Liverpool Street. That’ll give you away every time.
Caught the N87 last Saturday, and a guy was passionately trying to convince a stranger that pineapple on pizza is a crime. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a street musician playing a violin while a kid did breakdancing on the sidewalk, and everyone just stopped to watch. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a local cafe that serves coffee out of vintage gas station pumps. It's quirky and oddly nostalgic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who's local by their disdain for BART - they know to plan for delays like it's a second job. Tourists just look confused at the schedule.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 38 bus with a ukulele today while waiting for his ride. Only in SF do you get live music with your transit delays.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the boulevards, but the real charm hides in the tiny squares where locals gather for vermut and gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with their metro map, standing at the wrong exit while everyone else knows to look for the escalators.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when you hear someone ask if you want a 'half' on your drink – it’s not about measurements here.
Caught a lady on the 59 last night, singing old Motown hits. She had us all in stitches, but the driver just kept a straight face.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of teens joyfully transforming a vacant lot into a pop-up garden, sharing beer and laughter under a graffiti sunset.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a former airport turned park where you can spot rollerblading seniors racing against kite surfers on a Saturday afternoon.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a real local by how they know to avoid Dolores Park on weekends; the blanket chaos is a dead giveaway for tourists.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 3rd Muni bus in a row that had a wild saxophonist jammin’ in the back. Only in Bayview can that be the morning commute soundtrack.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and Plaza del Sol, but the real soul is in the forgotten streets where the old trades still quietly persist.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they fumble with the metro map, still figuring out that L5 connects to nowhere during rush hour.
